_meta.status: DEPRECATED — uses T60×60 profile. For T2" (50×50mm) specs see room-spec.json and panel-schema.json
_meta.description: Legacy spec file. Retained for reference and backward compatibility with older SVG/cost generators.

tbar_frame.name: T-bar profile — universal panel frame
tbar_frame.name_es: Perfil T — marco universal de panel

frame.description: One universal frame for all panel types (wall, floor, window, door, clerestory). Only infill varies.

long_side_tbar.name: Side T-bar
long_side_tbar.notes: Webs sit flush against adjacent panel webs when installed side by side. Continuous 3000mm structural spine. All cuts 90 degrees.

short_side_tbar.name: Top/Bottom T-bar
short_side_tbar.notes: 1000mm panel width + 60mm protrusion each end = 1120mm. Protrusion exposes full web (53mm height) for M12 bolt connections to connector ring. All cuts 90 degrees.

frame.notes: Panel is symmetric top/bottom — no 'this end up'. Same frame works as wall (vertical), floor (horizontal), or knee wall. One welding jig for all panels.

panel_to_panel_joint.notes: Adjacent panels' long-side webs press face-to-face along full 3000mm height. Massive shear plane. Clamped by connector rings above/below. Optional horizontal bolts for additional rigidity.

tbar_frame.notes: Standard Colombian T-bar (Gerdau Diaco, Aceros Arequipa). ONE frame design for ALL panel types. 6m standard stock: yields 2 side bars (3000mm each) with zero waste. Short bars: 6m stock yields 5 bars (1120mm each) with 400mm waste.

parametric_wall_system.name: Parametric wall thickness system
parametric_wall_system.name_es: Sistema parametrico de espesor de muro
parametric_wall_system.description: Wall thickness is dynamic, defined by the T-bar profile selection. All other system rules (frame geometry, bolt connections, connector ring, bamboo infill, mortar pour) remain identical regardless of profile size.
parametric_wall_system.thickness_formula: frame_depth = flange_width (e.g. 60mm for T60). finished_wall = frame_depth + 2 × (mortar_cover + lime). For T60: 60 + 2×13 = 86mm.
parametric_wall_system.key_insight: Frame depth equals flange width. Finished wall thickness for T60 is 86mm (60mm frame + 13mm mortar+lime per side). Larger profiles scale proportionally: T100 → 126mm, T150 → 176mm.

profile_definition.description: Any standard T-profile can be used. The system derives all geometry from these 4 input values. No hardcoded profile list — if the steel exists, the system works.

derived.total_height_mm: flange_width (for symmetric T) or flange_thickness + web_height
derived.weight_per_m: calculated from cross-section area × 7850 kg/m³ steel density
derived.protrusion_mm: = flange_width (short T-bars extend by 1× flange width each end)
derived.bolt_length: 2 × web_thickness + washers + nut (M12 for web ≤ 12mm, M16 for web > 12mm)

bolt_selection.notes: Bolt diameter scales with web thickness. Connection principle stays identical.

profile_definition.scaling_notes: The system scales from a garden shed (T 40×40, 66mm walls) to an 8-story building (T 200×200, 226mm walls). Frame depth = flange width. Finished wall = frame + 2×13mm mortar+lime. The panel frame geometry, connection logic, and assembly sequence are identical at every scale. Only the steel profile and bolt size change.

86mm.target: standard residential — uniform panel, minimum cost

what_stays_the_same: Frame geometry: 4 T-bars, 90 degree cuts, 90 degree welds
Protrusion: short T-bars extend 1 × flange_width beyond side T-bars on each end
Bolt: M12 through protruding webs (bolt length adjusts to web thickness)
Connector ring: same profile as panels, turned 90 degrees
Bamboo strips: same width/thickness, spacing adjusts to web height
Mortar: same recipe, volume scales with wall thickness
Assembly: identical process regardless of profile size
what_changes: Cost per panel (proportional to steel + mortar)
M12 bolt length (adjusts to web thickness: 2 × web_thickness + washer)

face_orientation.description: Every panel has two faces. Orientation (interior/exterior) is assigned at placement time, not fabrication. The frame is symmetric — any panel can face either way.

exterior_face.corner_attachment: rounded guadua quarter-culm wire-tied to protruding webs

interior_face.plaster: lime plaster, 10mm, fine finish for paint
interior_face.treatment: breathable interior paint or bare plaster
interior_face.electrical: PVC conduits against web, accessible from interior side

partition_panel.notes: Interior walls have no exterior face. Both sides get fine plaster.

floor_panel.top_face: walkable surface — terracotta tiles, teak flooring, or sealed mortar
floor_panel.bottom_face: visible ceiling — teak plank finish or exposed mortar with lime wash

parametric_wall_system.notes: The parametric system means a single specs.json + profile selection generates the complete building specification. A hotel project selects T 120×120 (150mm walls) and gets the same documentation, drawings, and assembly process as a casita with T 60×60 (85mm walls). Cost scales linearly. Stigma disappears at 150mm — indistinguishable from conventional masonry by feel and appearance.

bamboo_vertical_strip.name: Bamboo vertical strip
bamboo_vertical_strip.notes: Split guadua strips running vertically in wall panels. Screw-clamped at top and bottom every ~70 mm (every 2nd strip).

bamboo_diagonal_strip.name: Bamboo diagonal strip
bamboo_diagonal_strip.notes: Corner-to-corner diagonal, threaded through HDPE block notches at web level. Pre-tensioned. Forms X pattern (one per side, opposing corners). Wire-tied at every vertical crossing.

hdpe_block.name: HDPE spacer block
hdpe_block.notes: Spaces bamboo strips 30 mm from web at top/bottom of wall panels. Corner notches for diagonal entry. NOT suitable for floor panel support zones (creep under sustained load). Use in mid-span spacing only.

hdpe_notch.name: HDPE block corner notch
hdpe_notch.name_es: Muesca de esquina del bloque HDPE
hdpe_notch.notes: Cutout on web-facing side of HDPE block. Anchors diagonal strip at web level.

bamboo_arch_strip.name: Bamboo arch strip (floor panel)
bamboo_arch_strip.name_es: Tira de arco de bambú (panel de piso)
bamboo_arch_strip.notes: Forced into parabolic curve by graduated cross-pieces. 2 layers, wire-tied at crossings. Carries compression in the bowstring mechanism.

bamboo_tie_strip.name: Bamboo tie strip (floor panel)
bamboo_tie_strip.name_es: Tira de tirante de bambú (panel de piso)
bamboo_tie_strip.notes: Single layer, screw-clamped to bottom flange. 13 strips per side of longitudinal spine. Carries tension in the bowstring mechanism. Clamped with V-profile strips at support ends.

bamboo_cross_piece.name: Bamboo cross-piece (floor panel)
bamboo_cross_piece.name_es: Pieza transversal de bambú (panel de piso)
bamboo_cross_piece.notes: Perpendicular to span, sitting on T-bar web. Graduated height (0 at supports to 40 mm at midspan) forces arch into parabolic shape.

v_profile_clamp.name: V-profile clamping strip
v_profile_clamp.name_es: Tira de sujeción con perfil en V
v_profile_clamp.notes: Stamped V-tooth profile. Two strips sandwich the bamboo tie at each support end (teeth offset half pitch). Provides mechanical interlock, not just friction.

teak_end_block.name: Teak end block (floor panel)
teak_end_block.notes: Structural node at each support end. B-grade pieces and offcuts from structural teak — hidden inside mortar. Serves as arch bearing, V-strip mounting, bolt passage, and geometry anchor. No treatment needed (Class 1 natural durability).

mortar.name: Mortar
mortar.notes: Poured on vibration table (wall panels) or in place (floor panels). Pozzolan reduces pH, slowing bamboo degradation.

mortar_fiber.name: Fiber-rich mortar (upper zone, floor panel)
mortar_fiber.name_es: Mortero con fibra (zona superior, panel de piso)
mortar_fiber.material: Mortar + pasto estrella fiber (3-5% by volume)
mortar_fiber.notes: Used in upper zone of floor panels for acoustic damping (IIC improvement). Free invasive grass, millions of fiber-mortar interfaces absorb vibration.

rounded_corner.name: Rounded exterior corner
rounded_corner.attachment: wire-tied to protruding webs at corner
rounded_corner.mesh: chicken wire wraps continuously around the curve
rounded_corner.applies_to: all exterior building corners at all levels
rounded_corner.notes: Quarter culm provides geometry, chicken wire provides plaster key, hand-troweled lime creates organic curve. No two corners identical. Traditional bahareque aesthetic. Cost: ~$0 (offcuts + existing materials).

dense_bamboo_end_zone.name: Dense bamboo end zone (floor panel)
dense_bamboo_end_zone.name_es: Zona de extremo con bambú denso (panel de piso)
dense_bamboo_end_zone.notes: Pre-assembled bamboo block, wire-tied, cut ~10 mm oversize, press-fit into end zone above arch. Self-clamping. Replaces unnecessary mortar where shear is at only 5% utilization.

wire_tie.name: Galvanized wire tie
wire_tie.notes: At every diagonal-vertical crossing. Locks strips into rigid grid, distributes point loads, creates ductile failure mode.

pvc_conduit.name: PVC electrical conduit
pvc_conduit.notes: Between bamboo strips, against web. Protects 12V and 120V cables. Allows cable replacement without opening panel.

clamping_strip.name: Bamboo clamping strip (wall panel)
clamping_strip.name_es: Tira de sujeción de bambú (panel de muro)
clamping_strip.material: Guadua angustifolia or steel flat bar
clamping_strip.notes: Holds vertical bamboo strips against web via M5 screws every ~70 mm (every 2nd strip). One clamping strip at top, one at bottom.

connector_tbar.name: Connector T-bar (floor level)
connector_tbar.orientation: flange vertical (wall face), web horizontal (shelf into room)
connector_tbar.notes: Same T 60×60 stock, turned 90°. Runs full wall length at each floor level. Blue in SVGs to distinguish from wall (red) and floor (dark red) T-bars.

bolt_m12_connection.name: M12 connection bolt (universal)
bolt_m12_connection.notes: M12 × 30 mm through 14 mm of steel (two 7mm layers). One bolt size for all connections in the building.

bolt_spacing.name: Standard bolt spacing patterns
bolt_spacing.name_es: Patrones estándar de espaciado de pernos

wall_to_connector.notes: 100mm and 900mm from left edge of 1m panel. Bolt through protruding web (never encased in mortar) into connector flange.

floor_to_connector.notes: 250mm and 750mm from left edge of 1m panel. Bolt up through connector shelf into floor flange. Always accessible from room below.

protruding_web.location: top and bottom short T-bars, both ends
protruding_web.notes: Short T-bars (top/bottom) extend 60mm beyond side T-bars on each end. This exposes the full web face for bolting to the connector ring. The side T-bars (long) are flush — their webs contact adjacent panel webs directly.

connector_splice.notes: 50mm each side of panel joint line

bolt_hole_pattern.description: Universal bolt hole pattern for protruding webs. Parametric — scales with profile size. Drilled in the welding jig so every panel is identical.
bolt_hole_pattern.location: protruding web of short T-bars (top and bottom of panel), both ends

rules.edge_distance_min: 1.5 × bolt_diameter from any edge of the web

hole_positions.description: Holes positioned as a grid within the available area, respecting edge distance. Centered vertically on the web height.
hole_positions.vertical_position: web_height / 2 (centered — single row for small profiles, double row for large)

horizontal_positions.rule: evenly spaced within protrusion, starting at edge_distance from inner face (mortar boundary)
horizontal_positions.small_profile_1_bolt: 1 hole at protrusion / 2 from inner face. When flange_width < 3 × bolt_diameter × 2.
horizontal_positions.medium_profile_2_bolts: 2 holes at 1/3 and 2/3 of protrusion. When flange_width ≥ 3 × bolt_diameter × 2 and < 5 × bolt_diameter × 2.
horizontal_positions.large_profile_3_or_4: 3-4 holes evenly spaced. When flange_width ≥ 5 × bolt_diameter × 2. Also allows double vertical row.

vertical_rows.single_row: when web_height < 4 × bolt_diameter (e.g. T 60×60: 53mm < 48mm = false → still single row because 53mm allows only 1 centered hole with 18mm edge distance each side = 53-36 = 17mm usable, tight for 1 hole)
vertical_rows.double_row: when web_height ≥ 6 × bolt_diameter (e.g. T 150×150: 135mm ≥ 72mm → double row at 1/3 and 2/3 of web_height)

spacing_rule.description: Single horizontal row, centered vertically on web. Outermost bolts at edge_distance from panel face. Inner bolts evenly spaced between.
spacing_rule.v_position: web_height / 2 (always centered, single row, no vertical pairs)

heavy_pattern.when: multi-story (4+), high seismic, or engineer requires it

jig_drilling.description: All holes drilled in the welding jig BEFORE mortar pour. Jig has hardened drill bushings at the parametric positions. Same jig drills every panel identically.
jig_drilling.notes: Drilling after mortar pour is not possible — mortar encases the inner portion of the web. All holes must be pre-drilled. The jig guarantees alignment.

connector_ring_holes.description: Connector ring segments have matching holes at the same parametric positions. Drilled with the same jig bushings.
connector_ring_holes.alignment: Panel holes and connector holes align automatically because both use the same parametric rule.

foundation.method: flat slab → cure → saw-cut channels → DPC → drop panels → mortar fill
foundation.dpc: polyethylene membrane, laid in channel before panel
foundation.fill: self-leveling mortar after panel positioned and plumbed
foundation.notes: No teak liners, no formwork, no precision during pour. Flat slab is the simplest possible foundation. Channels cut after curing with panel-as-template marking. Same saw cuts both panel channels and drainage channels. Any wall thickness.

bolt_m12.name: M12 overlength bolt
bolt_m12.notes: M12 × 110 mm. Legacy spec — see bolt_m12_connection for the universal connection bolt.

screw_m5.name: M5 clamping screw
screw_m5.notes: Self-tapping in 7 mm steel (T 60×60). Every 2nd bamboo strip (~70 mm spacing, ~14 per clamping strip).

chicken_wire.name: Chicken wire mesh
chicken_wire.notes: Stapled to both panel faces on-site. Provides mechanical key for lime plaster adhesion.

lime_plaster.name: Lime plaster finish
lime_plaster.notes: Breathable, antifungal, self-healing. Optional: chopped dried grass fiber for crack resistance. Topped with lime wash.

cork_underlay.name: Cork underlay (floor panel)
cork_underlay.notes: Under terracotta tiles on floor panel top surface. Combined with pasto estrella fiber mortar achieves IIC 58-63.

epdm_gasket.name: EPDM gasket
epdm_gasket.notes: Used with M12 overlength bolts for waterproof seal at wall-floor connections.
